ground_truth.title: Skills in OpenAI Codex
ground_truth.author: Jesse Vincent
ground_truth.main_content: I've been working through some testing of OpenAI's new skills support in their codex CLI tool. Skills are currently gated behind a feature flag. You turn them on by running `codex --enable skills`. As I was trying to debug why a skill I'd installed wasn't triggering, Codex told me a bit about its' 'Discovery' skill. Claude Code finds skills in the `skills` subdirectories of installed plugins and the `skills` directory of your `.claude` directory at startup. It then shoves those into the system prompt as a list of available skills. At least the last time I looked, there was no way to enable specific skills for specific projects or subdirectories. It looks like Codex is doing something very different. It allows you to list additional skills in project documentation. OpenAI ship some 'global' skills in `~/.codex/skills/.system` - a 'plan' skill and a 'skill-creator' skill. You can drop your own global skills into `~/.codex/skills/`. And because it looks like they're just using a system prompt, the 'Discovery' meta-skill, and the 'File read' tool rather than custom skill tools, any sort of directory layout inside `~/.codex/skills/` works. They're just looking for any directory in that tree with a 'SKILL.md' file and going from there.
with: I've been working through some testing of OpenAI's new skills support in their codex CLI tool.
Skills are currently gated behind a feature flag. You turn them on by running `codex --enable skills`.
It looks like Codex is doing something very different. It allows you to list additional skills in project documentation.
OpenAI ship some 'global' skills in `~/.codex/skills/.system` - a 'plan' skill and a 'skill-creator' skill.
They're just looking for any directory in that tree with a 'SKILL.md' file and going from there.
